stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV slams US-Israeli war in Iran, demands peace talks
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV has strongly denounced the US-Israeli war in Iran, calling it a "delusion of omnipotence" and demanding that political leaders stop the violence and negotiate peace. In a statement to journalists, the Pope appealed for peace and rejected war, urging leaders to seek dialogue. This marks a shift in the Pope's tone, as he was initially reluctant to publicly condemn the violence when the war began six weeks ago. The Pope's comments come after the US President threatened to destroy "the whole Iranian civilisation". Pope Leo XIV is now explicitly calling for an end to hostilities and for political leaders to engage in peace talks, emphasizing the need to reject war and search for peace. His strongest words yet on the matter reflect a growing concern over the escalating conflict.

stories.headline: Year after Sindoor, Vance lands at Pakistan’s Nur Khan airbase
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance landed at Pakistan's Nur Khan airbase on Saturday, escorted by F-16 fighter jets, to lead American participation in talks with Iran aimed at ending the ongoing Gulf conflict. The airbase, located near Islamabad, was recently damaged by a missile barrage, with Pakistan's military stating that its air defense systems intercepted most of the incoming missiles. Despite this, a Reuters report cited an official who visited the base, stating that the barrage hit two roofs and a hangar of a refueling plane, while a geospatial analyst reported damage to two mobile control centers. The visit by Vice-President Vance shifts attention back to the key Pakistani strategic site, as he leads a US peace delegation in talks with Iran. The talks are part of efforts to resolve the ongoing conflict in the Gulf region.

stories.headline: Kenya Road Project Secures $550 Million World Bank Support to Improve Horn of Africa Trade
stories.summary: The World Bank has approved $550 million in funding to support the development of trade routes in Kenya, aiming to improve connectivity and trade in the Horn of Africa. The project will upgrade 508 km of the Isiolo-Mandera road, reducing travel time from 72 hours to 24 hours. Additionally, 1,270 km of fibre optic infrastructure will be installed to connect communities along the corridor. The road links Kenya to Ethiopia and Somalia, and is expected to lower the cost of transport and trade. The funding will benefit 3.2 million residents in Kenya's Northern region, one of the country's most underserved areas. The project is part of the World Bank's efforts to advance trade and development in the Horn of Africa, and will enhance regional connectivity and economic growth. The upgraded road will significantly improve travel time and trade efficiency.

stories.headline: Estonia allocates €120,000 in humanitarian aid to Lebanon
stories.summary: Estonia has allocated €120,000 in humanitarian aid to Lebanon, following a request from the Lebanese government to address the worsening humanitarian crisis. The aid will help provide essential supplies such as shelter, healthcare, food, and hygiene packs. Meanwhile, Estonia has also announced an additional €400,000 in humanitarian assistance to Ukraine, in response to Russia's continued strikes on the country's energy infrastructure. This move aims to support Ukraine during a period of severe cold weather. Ireland has also pledged €25 million in energy assistance to Ukraine. The aid allocations come as both countries face significant humanitarian challenges, with Estonia's contributions aiming to alleviate the suffering of civilians in both Lebanon and Ukraine. The European Union's initiative in emergencies has also supported the delivery of essential supplies to Lebanon.

stories.headline: France says no to Microsoft Windows and the reason is America; says: We want to regain control of
stories.summary: France is dropping Microsoft Windows in favor of Linux to reduce its reliance on American tech. The country plans to move its government workstations from Windows to the open-source Linux operating system. The decision is part of a broader European movement to decrease dependence on US technology. France wants to "regain control of our digital destiny" by relying less on US tech companies, citing concerns over data, infrastructure, and strategic decisions being controlled by external entities. The move aims to give France more control over its digital infrastructure, rules, pricing, evolution, and risks. This shift is seen as a step towards reducing the country's reliance on American technology and promoting digital sovereignty. The decision is in line with a growing trend in Europe to promote the use of open-source software and reduce dependence on US tech giants.

stories.headline: GTA 6 Dev Confirms Another Data Breach, Hackers Demand Ransom
stories.summary: Rockstar Games has confirmed another data breach, with a hacker group demanding ransom in exchange for not leaking sensitive information. The breach occurs ahead of the November 2026 launch of Grand Theft Auto 6 (GTA 6). Rockstar Games stated that there is no impact on their operations. The hacker group, previously involved in breaches of large companies like Microsoft and TicketMaster, is demanding payment. This incident follows a major breach that occurred in 2022. A deadline of April 14th has been set by the hackers, and more details are expected to emerge as the deadline approaches. Rockstar Games' official statement appears reassuring, but the situation is being closely monitored. The breach highlights the ongoing risk of cyber attacks and data breaches in the gaming industry. Further updates are anticipated as the situation develops.

stories.headline: 8 killer Android apps you won't find on the Play Store Who needs the Play Store?
stories.summary: Several Android apps are available outside of the Google Play Store, offering innovative and privacy-focused alternatives. F-Droid, a third-party app store, is a hub for these apps, providing a platform for installation and updates. Some apps, like Breezy Weather, can be downloaded from GitHub and sideloaded onto devices. Breezy Weather is a clean and simple weather app with a Material Design-inspired interface, free from ads and unnecessary features. These off-Play Store apps can be easily installed and updated using F-Droid or by sideloading them from GitHub. They offer a range of functionalities, from weather information to other utilities, and can be a great option for those looking for alternatives to traditional Play Store apps. F-Droid itself is a notable app, serving as a store for other off-Play Store apps.

stories.headline: Crimson Desert officially receives Intel Arc GPU support and XeSS 3 compatibility 23 days after launch
stories.summary: Crimson Desert has officially received support for Intel Arc GPUs and XeSS 3 compatibility, 23 days after its launch. The game can now be played on Intel Arc GPUs with improved performance and compatibility. The latest patch notes confirm official support, stating that compatibility and performance across various Intel GPUs will continue to be improved over time. Additionally, Intel XeSS 3.0 upscaling has been added as a new option under Settings, allowing for enhanced graphics quality. The update also includes Frame Generation, further enhancing the gaming experience. Developer Pearl Abyss has ensured that Crimson Desert is now compatible with Intel Arc GPUs, providing a better experience for players using these graphics cards. The official support is a significant update, addressing initial issues with artifacts and corrupted terrain, making the game more playable on Intel Arc GPUs.

stories.headline: Moran: Mayo improved from 'over-eager' start in Connacht SFC quarter-final victory
stories.summary: Mayo secured a comfortable win over London in the Connacht SFC quarter-final, advancing to the semi-final against either Roscommon or New York. Manager Andy Moran acknowledged that his team was "over-eager" at the start, attempting too many two-pointers. Despite this, Mayo delivered a solid performance, ultimately emerging victorious. The win marks a positive start to Mayo's championship campaign, following a semi-final exit last year with a nine-point defeat to Kerry. Mayo's improved performance bodes well for the future, with the team seeking to build on their past successes in the Connacht SFC, having won the title 13 times. The semi-final matchup against Roscommon or New York will be a crucial test for Moran's team.

stories.headline: How Bournemouth moved on from losing Antoine Semenyo
stories.summary: Bournemouth has extended its unbeaten run to a club record 12 Premier League games without defeat, despite losing star forward Antoine Semenyo to Manchester City in January. Semenyo scored in his final game for Bournemouth, a 3-2 win against Tottenham, before joining Manchester City for £65m. Since his departure, Bournemouth has not lost a game, with recent wins including a 2-1 victory over Arsenal at Emirates Stadium, courtesy of goals from Junior Kroupi and Alex Scott. The Cherries' unbeaten streak has boosted their European hopes and hindered Arsenal's title bid. Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola has expressed delight with his team's reaction to Semenyo's departure, with the side previously extending their unbeaten run to four matches with a 2-0 victory over Wolves. This streak has demonstrated the team's ability to move on from losing key players.

stories.headline: Sources: Hamby finalizing multiyear deal to remain with Sparks
stories.summary: Dearica Hamby, a three-time WNBA All-Star, is finalizing a three-year, fully guaranteed deal to remain with the Los Angeles Sparks. The contract is reportedly worth close to $3.5 million. Hamby, 32, joined the Sparks in 2023 via a trade with the Aces. This new agreement will keep her with the team for the foreseeable future. The deal is expected to be completed soon, according to sources who spoke to ESPN on Saturday. Hamby's return to the Sparks is a significant move for the team, securing the services of a key player. The contract details and terms have been confirmed by sources, indicating a long-term commitment between Hamby and the Sparks. The deal is a notable development in the WNBA, with Hamby set to remain a key part of the Sparks' roster.

stories.headline: An Issue Has Emerged With Nike Soccer Shirts Ahead of the World Cup
stories.summary: Nike's soccer shirts, set to be worn by players at the FIFA World Cup, have a design issue. The material has been seen bulging at the shoulders of footballers in recent international matches. This issue was raised by a sports journalist on social media. The problem has sparked attention ahead of the major tournament, where Nike's innovative designs are typically showcased. The company is known for its cutting-edge technology, such as the knitted football boots introduced at the 2014 World Cup. The current issue with the soccer shirts has raised concerns about the performance and quality of Nike's products, which are expected to be worn by top players on the global stage. The incident has drawn attention to the brand's designs and manufacturing processes.

stories.headline: 13 dead, 30 injured in major collision involving bus, truck in Bihar's Katihar Bihar chief minister Nitish Kumar announced an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh to the next of kin of each the deceased, and ₹50,000 to each injured.
stories.summary: A major collision involving a bus, a truck, and a pickup van occurred on NH-31 in Katihar, Bihar, resulting in 13 deaths and 30 injuries. The incident happened on Saturday evening in the Kodha block under the Korha Police Station jurisdiction. Local authorities and police immediately launched a rescue operation. Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar announced an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh to the next of kin of each deceased and ₹50,000 to each injured from the Chief Minister's Relief Fund. The accident prompted a swift response, with the chief minister providing financial assistance to the victims' families and the injured. The rescue operation was conducted promptly, and the authorities are providing support to those affected by the tragedy. The ex-gratia assistance is aimed at providing relief to the families of the deceased and the injured.

stories.headline: Drug smugglers’ licence, passport, Aadhaar to be revoked: J&K L-G Manoj Sinha J&K L-G Manoj Sinha announces strict measures against drug smugglers, including revoking licenses and passports, to combat substance abuse.
stories.summary: Jammu and Kashmir Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha has announced strict measures against drug smugglers, including revoking licenses and passports. The administration will confiscate property, revoke arms licenses, passports, Aadhaar, and freeze bank accounts of those involved in drug trafficking. A new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) has been issued, under which key documents like passports, driving licenses, and Aadhaar numbers of drug smugglers will be revoked. Sinha warned of asset seizure, Aadhaar cancellation, and other lifetime penalties for drug smugglers in J-K. The goal is to combat substance abuse and deliver swift punishment to those involved. The measures aim to prosecute ringleaders and seize assets, with a focus on swift delivery of punishment to curb drug trafficking in the region.

stories.headline: 'Only electric two-wheelers from April 2028': Delhi's draft EV policy is in, suggestions sought Government of NCT of Delhi has released a draft Electric Vehicle Policy 2026-2030, with deadlines for switching away from petrol and diesel vehicles.
stories.summary: The Government of NCT of Delhi has released a draft Electric Vehicle Policy 2026-2030, outlining deadlines for transitioning away from petrol and diesel vehicles. From January 1, 2027, only electric three-wheelers will be permitted for new registration in Delhi. Similarly, from April 1, 2028, only electric two-wheelers will be allowed for new registrations. The policy also proposes that at least 30% of school buses should be electric. The draft policy is open to public feedback for 30 days, seeking suggestions before implementation. The move aims to promote electric vehicles and reduce pollution in the city. Key deadlines include January 1, 2027, for electric three-wheelers and April 1, 2028, for electric two-wheelers, marking a significant shift towards electric transportation in Delhi.

stories.headline: TS Inter 1st Year Results 2026 Live: TGBIE 1st year marks memo soon to be active at tgbie.cgg.gov.in TS Telangana 1st year results 2026 live: The TS Inter 1st Year Results 2026 will be out on April 12, 2026, at 11 AM on the IE Education's site.
stories.summary: The Telangana Board of Intermediate Education (TGBIE) will announce the TS Inter 1st and 2nd year examination results on April 12, 2026, at 11 AM. Over 9 lakh students who appeared for Class 11th and 12th public examinations can check and download their marks memos via the official website: tgbie.cgg.gov.in. To access the results, students will need to enter their hall ticket number in the result login. The results will also be available on the IE Education's site and other portals like TOI and Manabadi. Students can expect to download their marks memos and check the pass percentage and topper list on the official website. The paper evaluation is complete, and the results are expected to be announced soon, allowing students to access their intermediate results.

stories.headline: US and China Engage in High-Stakes Diplomacy Amid Rising Tensions
stories.summary: The US and China are engaging in high-stakes diplomacy amid rising tensions. Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are expected to meet in South Korea's Gyeongju, with Beijing preparing by announcing new measures ahead of the talks. Recent geopolitical shifts, including a military pageant in China showcasing alliances with Russia and North Korea, have raised concerns among global leaders. The US has threatened a 100% tariff on Chinese goods, escalating trade negotiations between the world's two largest economies. Senior economic officials from both countries held five hours of talks in Stockholm to address unresolved trade issues, covering topics such as trade, Taiwan, technology, and bilateral ties. The meeting comes as tensions continue to rise, with both sides seeking an upper hand in the highly-watched negotiations. The talks aim to address key issues and ease tensions.

stories.headline: Russia-Ukraine Conflict Escalates as NATO Weighs In
stories.summary: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 175 prisoners of war each in a swap on Saturday, ahead of a planned ceasefire to mark Orthodox Easter. The exchange occurred as the conflict escalates, with Russia intensifying its air war and Ukraine targeting Russia's oil network. NATO is increasing its presence, with patrols in Polish airspace, amid concerns over the conflict's expansion. Estonia and Latvia have reported stray drones entering their territory, highlighting the war's growing reach. The conflict, now in its 1,304th day, is marked by a rhythm of intercepts and drone attacks. NATO members are on high alert, with Brussels engaged in quiet calculations to address the situation. The war continues to unfold, with both sides engaging in attacks and diplomatic efforts, as the international community monitors the situation closely.

stories.headline: Biden Administration Announces New Sanctions on North Korea
stories.summary: The Biden Administration has imposed new sanctions on North Korea, marking its first sanctions designations on the country. The move coincides with International Human Rights Day and targets the regime's human rights practices. However, experts believe the sanctions will have little impact on North Korea's human rights or nuclear and missile programs. The sanctions are largely symbolic, aiming to pressure the regime. The Biden Administration is still forming its strategy on North Korea, with consultations planned with US allies Japan and South Korea. Lawmakers expect policies on North Korea to be announced in the coming weeks. The move is seen as an attempt to assert pressure on North Korea, but its effectiveness remains uncertain. The administration's approach will be closely watched, particularly in light of criticism over its potential reliance on military force.

stories.headline: European Union Leaders Meet to Discuss Economic and Security Cooperation
stories.summary: European Union leaders met to discuss economic and security cooperation, focusing on strengthening ties with the African Union. The 7th EU-AU Summit, held in Luanda, Angola, aimed to enhance cooperation in areas such as trade, security, and sustainable development. The EU is also exploring ways to reduce dependence on external payment systems, with the development of Wero, a digital wallet created by European banks and payment companies. This initiative uses instant bank transfers, lowering fees and keeping data within Europe. The summit and related initiatives demonstrate the EU's efforts to bolster its economic and security position, addressing risks and promoting cooperation with other regions. The meetings and summits are part of the EU's ongoing efforts to strengthen its global presence and promote sustainable development.

stories.headline: Sensex, Nifty outlook for Monday, April 13: What to expect from stock market? Key levels, trading strategy & more
stories.summary: The Indian stock market is expected to open negatively on Monday, April 13, with Nifty futures down 291 points, or 1.26%, to 22,845.5. The domestic indices suffered a sharp fall, eroding around Rs 6.53 lakh crore in early trade. The Sensex dropped 931 points, while the Nifty was down 222 points, due to surging oil prices and lingering uncertainty around Iran-US tensions. The tensions weigh on market sentiment, particularly after Trump's fresh threats to Iran over the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route for oil supply. Experts advise adopting a hedged strategy and focusing on stock-specific opportunities, with portfolio allocation tilted towards fundamentally strong large-cap stocks, amid expected elevated volatility.

stories.headline: Indian Stock Market Witnesses Significant Downturn Due to Global Economic Uncertainty
stories.summary: The Indian stock market has witnessed a significant downturn due to global economic uncertainty. Global funds are dumping Indian equities at a record pace, driven by an energy shock from the US-Iran war. The top reasons for the downturn are weak global cues, fear of inflation, expected slowdown in industrial demand, and supply issues. Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) are exiting the market, while a weak rupee is adding to the woes. Global trade tensions and significant US policy shifts have also eroded investor confidence. As a result, the Nifty and Bank Nifty indices have been navigating a turbulent period, with a pronounced bearish sentiment and a multi-day losing streak. The Nifty50 and Sensex have dropped over 2%, hitting near two-month lows, signaling a cautious to bearish outlook for the immediate future. Experts predict a continued downturn due to these factors.

stories.headline: RBI Intervenes to Stabilize Rupee as Indian Stock Market Faces Volatility
stories.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has intervened in the forex market to stabilize the rupee, which has been facing depreciation pressure due to US-Iran tensions and other global volatility. The rupee's recovery is attributed to dollar selling by public sector banks, believed to be acting on behalf of the RBI. The central bank has intensified monitoring and liquidity management to stabilize the rupee and bond markets. The RBI's intervention aims to contain excessive volatility and prevent a disorderly depreciation in the rupee. The move comes as the Indian stock market faces volatility, with traders expecting the RBI to intervene periodically to maintain orderly conditions. The RBI's actions are also in response to concerns over potential US tariffs and their impact on the Indian economy. The intervention has helped contain volatility and stabilize the currency.

stories.headline: BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 Expected to Remain Under Pressure Amidst Ongoing Economic Slowdown
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 are expected to remain under pressure due to the ongoing economic slowdown. On Monday, the Sensex dropped 243.57 points to 77,319.33, while the Nifty 50 fell 173.25 points to 23,824. However, in a subsequent session, the Sensex jumped 787.30 points to close at 74,106.85, and the Nifty 50 gained 255.15 points to end at 22,968.25. The volatility is attributed to crude oil prices surging to nearly $100 per barrel, raising concerns about inflation and supply disruptions. Despite the gains, overall risk appetite remains cautious due to persistent inflationary pressures and concerns over potential disruptions to global trade. The market is also reacting to global developments, including the US-Iran ceasefire talks, which have led to fluctuations in the indices. The economic slowdown and global trade concerns continue to weigh on the market.

stories.headline: France says no to Microsoft Windows and the reason is America; says: We want to regain control of
stories.summary: France is moving to reduce its reliance on American tech giants by ditching Microsoft Windows. The country plans to transition some government computers from Windows to Linux as part of a broader push for digital sovereignty. French minister David Amiel stated that the effort is to "regain control of our digital tools" and reduce dependence on US technology. This move is part of a larger European movement to decrease reliance on American tech. France aims to promote digital autonomy and security by using open-source operating systems like Linux. The transition is expected to enhance the country's control over its digital infrastructure and reduce its vulnerability to external influences. The decision reflects a growing trend among European nations to prioritize digital sovereignty and reduce their dependence on American technology giants.

stories.headline: The crypto honeymoon is over for now as analysts warn of a major first-quarter profit squeeze
stories.summary: Coinbase and other cryptocurrency exchanges are expected to experience a major profit squeeze in the first quarter due to a decline in trading volumes. Volumes are estimated to have fallen roughly 30% from the prior quarter, resulting in lower revenue for the exchanges, which charge fees on each transaction. Barclays warned that absent a resurgence in near-term crypto trading activity, profitability will be under pressure at Coinbase. The slowdown is reflected in exchange data, with Coinbase's March trading volume being the weakest since September 2024. This decline in trading activity is expected to impact the company's profitability, leading to a downgrade of Coinbase's stock by analysts. The crypto market's weak start to 2026 has raised concerns about the industry's performance.

stories.headline: $1.6 billion Ether Machine SPAC deal collapses over unfavorable market
stories.summary: A $1.6 billion merger between Dynamix Corporation (DYNX) and The Ether Machine has been terminated due to unfavorable market conditions. The deal, announced in July 2025, aimed to take The Ether Machine public on the Nasdaq stock exchange under the ticker symbol 'ETHM'. The companies mutually agreed to end the agreement, citing unfavorable market conditions. As a result, Dynamix Corporation will receive a $50 million payment within 15 days. The termination ends The Ether Machine's path to the public markets via Dynamix Corporation. The decision was made effective immediately, with The Ether Machine citing unfavorable market conditions as the primary reason for the termination. The planned merger was initially intended to provide a public listing for The Ether Machine, an Ethereum treasury firm, but will no longer proceed.

stories.headline: yachts, golden visas and the Gilded Age: The startling escalation of Kiwi wealth
stories.summary: New Zealand is experiencing a significant escalation of wealth, dubbed the new Gilded Age. The ultra-rich are seeking unique ways to spend their money, including purchasing giga-yachts. To attract high-net-worth individuals, countries are offering "golden visas," which provide a fast track to residency and sometimes citizenship in exchange for substantial financial investments. This trend is part of a global phenomenon, with nations opening their doors to wealthy expats. New Zealand is also embracing golden visas, providing opportunities for the rich to gain residency and invest in the country. The growth of wealth in New Zealand is characterized by an increase in luxury spending and investment, with the wealthy seeking exclusive experiences and assets. This shift is transforming the country's economy and society, with implications for inequality and social change.
